channel.title: SCP Foundation - new forum posts
channel.description: Posts in forums of the site "SCP Foundation" - Secure, Contain, Protect

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello Gruvint8! Welcome to the Wiki! I'm Ralliston, a site author and a member of the Community Outreach Team - with this helpful little comment, I'll try to help you find your way around the site and link a bunch of useful places and links that will help you in your SCP journey.

It's awesome to see someone with prior writing experience joining the wiki - I'm sure what you'll come up with during your stay here will be absolutely amazing. I can't wait to see your stuff. It's also great to see that after all of these years "lurking", you finally decided to make an account on-site. You'd be surprised at how many people stay only as lurkers and never properly become members. After all, now you can actively affect the quality of articles posted on-site by voting, you can comment, and, most importantly it seems, contribute.

Without further ado though, let's click this collapsible, shall we? :)

+ show block
– hide block

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our Sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts, which are in-works articles. Using it, you can link the drafts to reviewers to get their opinions on them before posting. It really helps everyone out a lot.

For brainstorming concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by experienced members of the community if you need help refining and fleshing out your concept. It's a very helpful little place for when you want to start your writing journey, especially when you're not sure if the idea is all that great.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles. Within the Guides, you will find useful information written by experienced members of the site for the use of newer ones.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success! Remember - there is no rush to writing - take your time! The more time you spend on fleshing your ideas out, the more your chance of success becomes!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community to implement into your very own writing. Alternatively, you might want to look at the User Curated Lists page, where members of the community list and rank their favorite articles in different genres and themes, showing which attempts at such are successful and what the community likes.

Best of luck with your future contributions to the site! I hope you enjoy your time here as much as we do! If you have any more questions, feel free to message me here on Wikidot or on the IRC chats!


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

Deceptively engrossing. Zigs when you expect it to zag. Nicely done.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5195

The article is ok, though in many aspects could be improved, the foundation takes on a clinical tone in articles and idioms are usually not used in the tone, clinical tone is meant to be specific.

The rosy glasses are interesting but it is kind of like a regular anomalous object, the object just gives a rush of dopamine but it is just a pair of magical glasses that makes the wearer happy and doesn't really warrant an article, just contained on one of the security lockers with the key being held by a researcher and having an entry in the list of anomalous objects

I still believe that there's potential in here though, maybe add to it more? Take it more in a direction of your choosing.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5127

item.title: Re: The Laughing Tree
item.content:encoded:
Hi RED, Thank you for the review.

Some concept details couldn't be included since I hit the 400-word limit, but many of your points are valid. I'll address them below.

+ show block
– hide block

how exactly does the tree convince you to tie someone up?

It targets socially isolated kids. It frames the victim for theft. And this part was removed, but the tree also trips aggressive kids while they are near this victim. (Also, some members of the camp staff are in on it, as they are former victims)

does the tree itself carve the child's name? or do the children do it?

The tree does it.

how exactly do these victims live on to help the tree? do they still have any free will? what happens when they leave the camp? what exactly happens after ones name is carved?- in the immediate future?

They leave with a warped sense of empathy, and enjoy watching other kids go through the same ordeal. Some of them return as camp staff and help "stoke the fire" by laughing at the victim when he asks for help or by putting a stolen item back when the victim tries to get rid of it.

why the child didn't want to go to camp

Prior to camp, he was already socially isolated among peers.

what exactly happened after he got tied to the tree.

The interviewed child is not tied to the tree. He manages to stop its influence by wetting himself. One particular goal of this piece is to show that those that escape still feel shame and have a normal sense of empathy. The interviewed camp staff, however, was tied to the tree in his youth, and now laughs at the experience while disregarding the pain of others.

parts of the narrative that show the parent stepped in?

The parent is not present at the camp. He only steps in during the interview, when his child becomes distraught or to answer the few questions he can.

Regarding your point about how the tree is called to the Foundation's attention. It's true that this is a little lacking. But I think that the foundation would be interested in rumors/tales that allude to a possible SCP entity. Perhaps the investigation department checks the background for names written on the tree and finds a pattern of anti-social behavior among them. Is there something in SCP that could possible help with this point? like a signature for a nefarious group or individual?


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Laughing Tree

What do I see in this object? An anomaly and a fairly predictable joke. Yes, the joke is not bad and the design is chosen perfectly, but it is still just a joke and there is nothing behind it. I agree with some of the commenters above, it could very easily be a -J, but this is in the main series.
If you compare this to the very similar in my opinion SCP-3078, you may understand the reason for my disappointment.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5922

item.title: Re: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback. And no, he doesn't look like The Mask at all. The trickster isn't actually for acting in ways like a clown or a cartoon character. The trickster I'm describing isn't chaotic but enjoys joking and helping others at the same time shown to be always carefree, immature, goofy, unpredictable, and comes off as a slight irresponsible idiot. But sometimes, the character can be serious in some situations.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Thanks by the way in the additional notes it says the effects happen till there are no more memories to be erased even if you take the pom poms off.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]

item.title: (seeking greenlight/feedback) a mysterious entity
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Mystery

Elevator Pitch: A mysterious female being with some anomalys about her like an undefinable age and a bad forecast for the future of humankind.

Central Narrative: The main database entry acts as a brief introduction and explanation to the viewer, the main narrative really happens in the interviews with the SCP. The main point of the story is the entity's forecast of THEM, which are never rally explained and leave room for interpretation.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The entitys character. Especially in the interviews, the entity seems humorous and lively.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (seeking greenlight/feedback) a mysterious entity

item.title: Criticism and Review
item.content:encoded:
Huzzah! I am finally ready to begin work on my first SCP!

I do not require green lights as I already have a +1 post on the main site (here)

Here it is:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/doggols-sandbox-ii

Under the tab 'Criticism and Review:**

My concerns:

  • Weirdness - Is it too hard to understand, can the mystery be solved?
  • Length - Is it too long or too short?
  • Story - Is the story behind it good enough?
  • Consistency - Is the anomaly consistent?

Fair warning, this is a Department of Misscomunications SCP, so It will be a bit confusing to readers. This is intentional.

spoilers, only click after reading
hide

Basically, a group of distinctly non-human individuals are messing with the Australian legal system. They've been using it to strike money out of people who break their laws copyright, and anyone who notices they're not human gets arrested for slander. These entities are incredibly tall, and leave a lot of mess wherever they go.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Drafts Critique
Forum thread: Criticism and Review

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Good day everyone,

I'm a long time fan of SCP and have read my fair share of articles through the years. But since the start of corona I started getting into writing. I had allot of SCP ideas but was never sure about sharing them. Then in this past week, an idea of an SCP came to mind and I wrote it down. And after letting my close friends read it I was getting more comfortable with sharing my writing and finally decided to join. I hope to get better at writing through here and to just have an amazing time in this lovely community!

Regards,
TurtwigTheSpy (Some random Dutch Lad)


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

item.title: (Seeking greenlights and tips) An army of darkness
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Horror, Other: Discovery

Page Layout (Optional): The page will have some test logs, addendums and audio logs

Elevator Pitch: In Antarctica a dark portal to another dimension suddenly appears, after some scientists get lost in Antarctica the foundation started an investigation. That is when they found the dark warriors. Lead by a general corrupted by darkness.

Central Narrative: After the foundation finds the portal and the creatures a provisional site is built around the portal. The SCP includes two entities, the general and his soldiers (proxies). The general commands his proxies or the proxies act on their own attacking any life-forms not "Embracing the dark". The general and his proxies are dark they don't have: skin, skeletons or even organs. the general is rarely seen his soldiers are mostly in our dimension and not in the "Dark" dimension. Both the soldiers and the general can corrupt biological life forms by killing them, when a human is killed a dark goo consumes them and turns them into a soldier but if the body is too damaged or did not die by the hands of the entities no transformation will happen. All dark entities have a severe case of Photophobia1 since light can hurt and melt them. Inside the portal there seems to be a world which had a SK-Class Dominance Shift Scenario where the entities took over earth in that dimension but humans still survived and are near extinction. The foundation also found the general is building an army which could result in a BI Class Pre-Invasion Scenario.

+ Show more
- Show less

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The attention grabber here is the army. It's goals are yet unknown and it's size is large and feared. I think readers would want to continue reading to know the mysterious outcome of the article.

Additional Notes: None

Footnotes
1. Fear of light

Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (Seeking greenlights and tips) An army of darkness

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi everybody, my names Phil and I'm just here chasing my joy.

I'm a recent University graduate who after some self evaluating recognized that the aspect of my degree I enjoyed most was the storytelling. I've always been a fan of the SCP universe and the way its many stories are spun together into an ever expanding universe of many minds. So I thought, where better to practice writing than here?

I'm a massive fan of collaborative story telling, a result of my immersion in ttrpg culture and nerdom, and really wanted the experience of discussing narrative with a community. This will be some of my first writing uploaded to a large public space and I find myself both excited and nervous to take the plunge. Thanks so much for anytime you might've spent on my ramblings and I hope to talk to and share my work with people soon. :)


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

item.title: Re: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.
item.content:encoded:
My concern is that this feels too narratively close to the mask still; like the effects are clearly based off that; but so is a lot of the character and conflict here too, even if the actual way this plays out is quite different. What I mean is that Michael is basically a straight rip of Jim Carrey's character, with like, the same 'wants to help but is chaotic' vibe as the mask has already and it feels…very derivative.

Beyond this, I don't actually have a good idea of how this works: you mention the big twist is that michael is actually real sad and that's not an awful idea but I don't really get how you're like, intending building up to it? And I'm really not convinced it's enough of a twist to carry this: it kinda feels like it'd be better being very upfront with it and then really exploring like, the dichotomy of michael wanting everyone to be happy and like trying to help and him just being super depressed and hating life.

I also don't really have a good idea of what the core plot beats you want to hit are: there's like, an obvious opening where you introduce his powers, and then…I have no idea? You refer to interviews and some logs but it feels like they're expository regarding his powers over like, actually telling a story and imo that will make them really boring to read.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.

item.title: Re: (Seeking Greenlight) Mirror Cyborgs
item.content:encoded:
I guess my central concern with this is that you have a really interesting setting for the story but I'm not really sure what you're like…actually going to do with it? Like, I'm not hugely familiar with Anderson so I'll defer to Conwell in terms of how well it fits into that canon; but my concern here is just…how do you link the pieces up?

Like, the idea that for every Gyrfalcon, there's a cybernetic doppleganger hunting down the human user is fine but what are you doing with this? How does the Broken God stuff tie into this article? How do the initial pair of cyborgs tie in?

My concern is that it feels like the stuff about an assassin android attempting to hunt down a human with a robot arm is by far the most interesting part of this pitch and that nothing else really builds off that well. Maybe it'd be best writing it as a tale from the perspective of one of the two cyborgs where you can like, really explore their motivations and have cool action sequences as they nearly but don't quite get their target or w/e?


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (Seeking Greenlight) Mirror Cyborgs

item.title: Re: Your friendly neighborhood demon
item.content:encoded:
I feel like you've definitely got a fun setting for a story here; but I don't really have a strong feeling for what you're actually like, doing here? Specifically: it seems like you're spending a lot of time interviewing the SCP and like showing off how amusing they are but my concern is that this doesn't really like… go anywhere? There's no big emotional or narrative arc within these interviews and so you're left kinda relying on them being interesting enough alone to carry this and…I'm not sure they will be?

There's definitely elements I like e.g. the exploration of the like, kinda paranoia you get working at the Foundation if they're willing to kill you for breaking a rule; and the whole angle of the demon being so annoying hell is glad they're gone; and I just…kinda want to see you tell more of a complete story around these and really use these elements to…do something?


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Your friendly neighborhood demon

item.title: Re: The Dark Lantern
item.content:encoded:
[+] Open for crit
– hide block

So, I was thinking that for the story, someone would find a journal of the creature in that dimension and based on the notes there he would discover that the creature was once a human but it was transformed into this anomaly after having found this lantern. He became the lantern bearer and was corrupted by it.

My personal feeling, and other reviewers may disagree, is that backstory like this is almost always the wrong approach to take in writing a SCP: the core issue (for me) is that the Foundation are writing this article, and so it's easier to get a reader invested if the Foundation actively take a protagonistic role in the story (i.e., they are the PoV telling the story, their decisions are the ones that should actually affect it). I don't necessarily dislike this as a start but imo you should take this and run with it and explore how the SCP feels know their the lantern bearer and how that's affected by the Foundation coming into view.

Uhhh, I forgot to mention it’s an oil lantern. It needs oil to stay lit, not wood. When it transforms you, you’ll become a tree full of oil. Then it will use that oil to fuel the lantern.

I'm a little torn on this because on the one hand, it still feels very complex but on the other, I guess it's kinda how rubber trees work.

I was thinking that it would add some nice background information, you know, make things more realistic, more believable.

Sure but you're trying to tell a cohesive short story here, adding in stuff that adds to the realism isn't necessarily a good idea if it doesn't add to the core of the story being told.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Dark Lantern

item.title: SCP 5334 idea
item.content:encoded:
My idea is to make a twist on the traditional story of the monster under the bed. SCP-5334 will be a medium size creature with a elongated face. SCP-5334 appears to be cut in half but shows not signs of bleeding, SCP-5334 walks around on it two drills it has for arms. When SCP-5334 finds a bed/any type of sleeping thing elevated above the ground at least 30 cm it will attach its lower half, to the bottom of the bed, when the user falls into the 4th stage of sleep/the deepest sleep it will wake the user which we will now refer to as SCP-5334-1 and put SCP-5334-1 into Sleep paralysis then proceeding by using its drill like arms to make two 8 cm by 8cm holes in the lower chest. It will then proceed by eating the Heart and organs of SCP-5334-1 before proceeding to the next victim.

This is a small description of the scp, i hope you can get back to me with thing i can change or make better.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP idea seeking greenlight/help!!!

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Hello my thoughts exactly I was going to change the order since I wasn't 100% sure I could change the order of sections

You, technically can in that there's no rules against it; but it's a real bad idea to do so just to actually tell this story well.

How about I show how much happiness she had by showing everybody dies in the town she is in from malnutrition?

This doesn't change the core issue; which is that the progression of the article is eminently predictable as soon as we've finished reading the description. What you really need to do is to figure out a subversion of the predictable somehow.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]

Definitely my favorite GOI, and the hub is great and makes it very easy to understand their motivations which can be difficult to come by when so many things here are so mysterious. Like, sure, that's cool and all, but some of us want to understand things without having to find all the info scattered about through 15+ different articles. So this is great, +1.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: Serpent's Hand Hub

item.title: Dr. Ethan Millan (SCP and researcher idea)
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes
Genre: Drama/Emotional
Type: article, later used in tales
Dr. Ethan Millan is a 26 years old researher, who was trying to invent time-travel. After a few testruns on his machine, the mechanism exploded, taking his maker into another reality. He crashlanded into an O5 meeting. After he was questioned, it was clear, that he was from another reality (interview oppotrunity), and because he saw the O5s, he was given amnestics. Surpriseingly, it didn't work. After few tests, it became clear, that he somehow has immunity to seemingly all anomalous effects. Hes knowledge and intelligence has proven to be valuable, and after he has failed to recreate the accident he was in (different physics), he has requested to work in the foundation. After a psychic evaluation, it was clear that he will not give up the identity of the O5 council. His request was accepted, but he was under a strict survillance. He was testing Safe class objets, specifically which had some memetic effects (as he was immune). Later, he died in a containment breach. His corpse immideatly disappeared, and his body reanimated 3 minutes later, in the same position and state as he arrived into this universe. The compromised meetingroom was obviously abadoned, but he was discovered by some patrolling facilityguard wandering around curiosly. At this time, his class was updated from safe to euclid (may need help because I don't know how to do this). They explained him what happened (leaving out the classified bits) and he was accepted back as a researcher. The meeting room was reassigned as his personal arriving chamber, after dicovering, that after his death, he always back to that point. A few Keter testing was made with him, many of which resulted in his death (he is only immune to anomalous effects, not physical like breaking your bones). Later keter testing was only allowed, if it was really necessary, and if he has agreed with it (as he really dies, when he dies). The time between death and reanimation is usually between 30 seconds and 5 days.

My intention with this character are that some scp objects can be further explored, as they couldn't be studied. I intend to create some drama in tales, as his memories also die with him (some love affair with his assigned researher assistant, because he needs someone to fill in the blanks after he dies). Obviously I will create a normal article with containment procedures, but I need to know if this idea is too much or not. I'm open to new ideas. This character was inspired by Doctor Who's captain Jack Harrkness, just to be clear. The whole idea is that he is stuck in the timeline.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Dr. Ethan Millan

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Author, per the required reading forum guidelines: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282684/required-reading:how-to-use-this-forum-ideas please fill out the concept template to seek feedback on your idea.

Remember to please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of your first comment rather than creating a new thread.

Once you have revised your post accordingly, please reach out to reviewers to request that they look at your thread. You can find a list of ways to contact reviewers in this thread: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews

(Also, that said… you might want to look through some of the character-writing information pages in the Comprehensive Guide Hub if you haven't yet already. The character you have here is already likely to be scrutinized since it seems like a self-insert due to the name being the same as your account name, and the setup of "anomalous researcher hired by Foundation keeps their anomaly a secret" can make the Foundation look incompetent and the character too perfect if not handled carefully.)


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]

item.title: Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
I've had this character in my mind for a while and have been working on a tale for her I would like to publish. Without explaining everything about her, she is a doctor of psychology who works (mostly) with low-threat humanoid SCPs, evaluating their mental states within containment and helping the Foundation better straddle the line between "this is cold but necessary" and "this is unreasonably cruel".
And because this is the Foundation, she has a minor anomalous ability. Extending from various points on people's bodies she can see strings of light no wider than a spider's thread extending, and these threads connect to other people based on familial relation. For example, siblings are all linked by threads extending from their inner left wrists. It's not a story with many practical implications, but in a workplace where knowing something you aren't supposed to can be extremely dangerous she feels very fortunate that she's kept her ability secret since childhood.
The main basis of this little tale is that I want to write about some of her observations and discoveries and the struggles that this knowledge comes with. On one hand a lot of the information she has could be very useful (if SCPs are related to each other, if ones assumed to be 'created' like the Little Misters have signs of parentage or families), but on the other hand knowing intimate family situations of Important People in the Foundation may not be something said people are much of a fan of. And then on a third hand, sometimes she just longs to be gossipy about it all.
It's not an extremely long story, only about 1500 words and mostly completed, but it expresses a lot of important things regarding Olivia's character that I might want to explore more in other ways.

Okay thanks for reading I sure hope I did this right!


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]

This article is good, it's wholesome and it's a cool article. It is short but I think that adds to the charm of the article, it is a nice break from the other articles and is nice that a scp is doing good in the world without foundation intervention

Just looked at the header (the pictures of those symbol things at the top) and it's pretty distorted on my laptop, will check if it's a hardware bug or not

+1


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5699

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Man it's been a while since I've written any kind of introduction post.
Hi- I'm Leah. I originally just joined this Wiki because I wanted to read the SCP-4231 article and it is (rightfully so) hidden behind content warnings. But I've had a character idea for a while and so I started working on a tale surrounding her not long ago. Hopefully I'll become happy enough with it that I'll risk baring my creative soul to a bunch of strangers on a forum, which is an absolutely terrifying concept and I mean that with no sarcasm because I'm an anxious mess :)


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
Here Lies HarryBlank. He did The Password and Tiamat.

I'm glad you liked them, and I'm glad you let me know! Thanks much.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5109

item.title: Re: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
The ballet dancing as a fighting style is something that only the automaton does

I know. It doesn't make sense for the automaton to do it. It's overly flowery and kind of useless in combat—there's no need for flourishes or taking the time to do elegant poses in a street brawl. Or does this thing only get into fights if they're long, drawn out movie-style shows of acrobatics?

+ show block
– hide block

I'm really confused as to how I can improve this and I accept some help. If it really has to be deleted, I'll be sad, I admit, but I want to make this work.

Again, you need to figure out what the reason for the automaton to exist is. If it's just trying to be a "perfect warrior", then you'd need to remove the gratuitous ballet stuff. It wouldn't make sense to sacrifice viability of technique for aesthetic—all of the examples you've mentioned are fictional animated characters, who don't need to be believable in terms of real-life maneuvering. If the automaton is trying to better itself through study of the arts, it makes more sense to keep that in.

the are almost guaranteed to be warriors as the directive for the SCP to change its host is killing the previous host

It doesn't take a warrior to kill someone.

or (if the host is already dead) defeating the SCP in battle

Again, all sorts of underhanded ways this very simple requirement can be exploited. Unless you've got some additional requirements to slap on that require "honorable combat" or the like, which just feels even more cliche and contrived.

About the mnestics/amnestics, the SCP is somehow a "backup file".

My question is why. You've already got a lot of kind of random details, abilities, and restrictions that don't really match for the automaton; why does it need to be a backup file too?

Right now the automaton and the field agent seem like two characters from different stories, and all the accompanying details just overcomplicate the narrative; I say pick one to focus on and remove most of the minutiae from the other.

it is discovering the SCP rules, how it works and why it works this way. It seems simple, but there is this conundrum about exposing too much or too little of the SCP.

When discussing concept, you always want to answer the reviewer's questions, even if it means "exposing" material. You do not help yourself at all by intentionally keeping things secret from the reviewer; reviewers will not automatically assume that what they don't know about the piece is automatically good.

If you don't have anything planned for why this automaton exists the way it does, just say that. If you do have a backstory for why it acts this way, tell the reviewers so we can let you know if we think it's a good story or not.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)

item.title: Re: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
I'm very happy with your reply as it helps a lot. There somethings that I've notice I HAVE to make clear and some that I have to correct based on your criticism. I'll take notes above as I'm going to sleep now, but I wanted to answer as fast as possible.

+ show block
– hide block
  • The ballet dancing as a fighting style is something that only the automaton does, not the host. As you've said, it would be very difficult and hurtful to a human to do that. As the automaton is metallic and resistant, it would not hurt itself with it. I've imagined it fighting style not as ballet itself, but something as Pearl and Mega Pearl (Steven Universe), Fi (Zelda) and Bon Clay (One Piece), something based on the dance, but more aggressive, using flexibility and precision. I'm really confused as to how I can improve this and I accept some help. If it really has to be deleted, I'll be sad, I admit, but I want to make this work.
  • The "common agent" thing is a communication error. It is a Foundation member anyway and took part in a mortal battle with a metallic sword lady. I'm having issues leveling it as I don't want the host to be a superhero but they are not an "everyman" stock character.
  • The metallic human form is the SCP's "defense mode". I didn't want the SCP to be a floating defending sword as there are already some floating weapon SCPs, so I've created this metal ballerina that is the host bastion while it is incapable of fighting by itself. If it really becomes an issue, I think it can be changed.
  • About the host, the are almost guaranteed to be warriors as the directive for the SCP to change its host is killing the previous host or (if the host is already dead) defeating the SCP in battle. Both are very hard as both are based in fighting with the aforementioned "Ultimate Warrior"/"Ultimate Weapon".
  • About the mnestics/amnestics, the SCP is somehow a "backup file". It's not as the SCP is resisting the amnestics, but it is recovering the host's memory. Is as if someone deleted everything in their computer, but there is an external HD with a backup that keeps being used to restore the computer data. The SCP is resilient to amnestics as it is a sword, it is inorganic, maybe an AI that the Foundation can't figure out how to reprogram. I don't really know if amnestics work on inorganic objects and if it works, I'll rethink this part.
  • Finally, about the SCP's backstory there is the mnestic experiment part. As the host is visiting previous owners' memories, it is discovering the SCP's rules, how it works and why it works this way. It seems simple, but there is this conundrum about exposing too much or too little of the SCP.

Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)

I really, really liked this story. The bit at the end was especially well-written and the imagery was wonderful, and reminded me a lot of "The Last Question" by Isaac Asimov.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: Falling Into Stars

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Greetings everyone! I am a beginner at writing so I decided to write SCP because:
1- I love the SCP lore
2- I have an ambition to be a renown writer
Anyway i look foward on working with you guys and knowing you all! I will already start working!

P.S- I'm Brazilian. Come to brazil please?


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

Maybe we shouldn't take that guess, 2021 is still as insane as 2020 with the current number of crises being:

-2-

Crises 1: Covid-19
Crises 2: Nuclear treaty expiring

-Please reply to ask for more crises-


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-1032

item.title: The Personaphage (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Type: SCP article

Genre: Horror

Elevator Pitch:

An entity that appears to its victims in weird recurring dreams. Once it has chosen a victim, the entity wil subject them to personalized dreams designed to trick them into consenting to having their personality eaten. A victim who has gone through this process will loose memories, distinctive character traits and their overall sense of self. At the end of the process, the victim is left an empty shell (no memories or personality whatsoever). The entity has guided humanity to consciousness and organized society in order to harvest and eat our personalities.

Central Narrative:

Through interviews with the victims and a dream journal the foundation pieces toghether who/what is the entity. At the end the foundation deduces that the entity feeds off personalities. We also discover that the victims are persuaded in their dreams to consent to being eaten through manipulation and deception. It seems that this entity is connected to the rise of consciousness in humans who has bred this trait in us like cattle to harvest. The foundation must then supply a more or less steady stream of tasty D-class in order to keep all victims accounted for.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:

In this entry, what makes humanity special on planet earth is simply something this entity bred in humans through our dreams in order to hunt us for sport and sustenance. The entity itself also will have an unsettling atmosphere using its victims memories, fears and quirks in order to "eat" them.

Additional Notes:

Containment procedures:
-Finding eligible D-class and feeding them to the entity.
-Finding and dealing with any other victims outside of foundation control.

There will be an MTF involved in the article that specializes in "onironautics" (advanced lucid dreaming).

The entity targets people who have led emptionally intense lives prefering those who have some kind or remorse since they are generally easier to manipulate in dreams.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Personaphage (Seeking Greenlight and Feedback)

item.title: Re: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
Got a PM about this, took a look.

I'll be honest, this seems like a mishmash of unrelated elements picked because they kind of sound cool rather than because they work together to tell a coherent story. The inclusion of ballet, for example, seems extremely forced since not only do professional ballerinas injure themselves all the time for their art, ballet is (at least classically, if I recall correctly) meant to be performed to music, with pre-determined choreography. It would be a lot more work to try and adapt ballet to combat than, say, having someone train in self-defense techniques that are easier to learn if one has the flexibility of a principal dancer. (This admittedly reminds me of Pearl from Steven Universe.)

+ show block
– hide block

There's also the issue that the automaton's existence doesn't really make sense. If it wants to become a "perfect weapon", why does it bother with incorporating ballet into its fighting skillset? If it's looking for a "perfect warrior", why is it obligated to protect hosts who aren't guaranteed to be warriors at all?

I start losing interest immediately at:

They are an common field agent that was in the wrong place at the wrong time. This host will discover SCP “memory flash drive" powers and turned into the subject of an mnestic experiment to discover the SCP memories.

This sounds like a generic anime protagonist or the like: they start off as "common" but just happen to encounter an event that gives them a spontaneous superpower that conveniently makes the rest of the story possible. I find myself extremely skeptical that this agent not only happens to defeat the automaton, they also conveniently obtain powers for revealing extra information spoonfed-exposition-style. It feels contrived and doesn't make for a good story because it feels too convenient. Even the emotional element with the memory feels clumsily manipulative—having the amnestics not work because "the automaton just does what it does" is rather unsatisfying as a story point.

if you think the SCP is weak for being focused on its "owner''.

I feel like the issue is more that this just feels like a rehash of Neon Genesis Evangelion, except the pilot's backstory is much blander and the associated automaton makes a lot less sense in-universe. If you really want to try to make this work, I feel like you're going to have to better develop the automaton first, since right now it seems to exist solely to facilitate this specific sob story about some random powered-up field agent. The audience isn't likely to take this seriously until you make the core anomaly more unique and narrative-driven rather than just a conglomerate of disparate aesthetics. Why does the automaton exist the way it does? How can you make that more meaningful to the average reader, who is very much a normal human with no powers and no resistance to amnestics?


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)

item.title: Re: any suggestions for my SCP?
item.content:encoded:
Hello LongJumpingMan! let's take a look :)

SAND

+ show block
– hide block

The concept:
Elevator Pitch: someone who was a regular person now considered something dangerous due to his ability to record audio with his mind and play the way he heard it out of his mouth sounding exactly the same.

While this does sound doable, I have trouble believing this would cause to him to be considered a danger. While the actions he could do have the potential to be dangerous, he himself isn't really a threat (at least not from what described).

Along with this, is there anything that really expands on what this is, or why it happened to him. Currently the skip feels like "your average guy, but with a cool, unexplained power", as it doesn't seem like theres much behind the initial effects.- will the possible origins or purpose of the voice disc be elaborated on at all?

The narrative:

Central Narrative: a person who was once a regular man woke up and looked in the mirror to discover a weird circular device stuck in his neck he tried to pull it off but couldnt.
he tried to speak but then discovered he could sound exactly like singers of popular songs after singing scatmans world and even able to sing instrumental songs with his throat.
for 3 months he kept this secret from friends and family.
eventually the Foundation caught wind of this after investigating news papers from the subjects hometown about his ability and then later caught him after he was heard singing Michael Jacksons song thriller.

I second [[*user Grigori Karpin] in terms of the narrative, there isn't anything that shows what exactly happens after the skip gets contained. The narrative currently feels unfinished due to it being cut short right after the setup.

The narratives cut off also makes the skip itself feel a bit flat, as there isn't any time to really develop him or his ideals. There isn't much to get invested in here. What the narratives main conflict? what takes place after the skip is contained? whats everything building up to? how will the skip and its ideals affect the narrative?

Red or green?
Due to my afore mentioned concerns, no-greenlight.

If you would like me review this (or any concept) further, then please feel free to send me a PM or ask via IRC. Response times generally range from 2-12 hours over PMs. If your looking for additional critiques/perspectives in general, then I would highly recommend contacting the butterfly squad :)

Cheers,

RED


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: any suggestions for my SCP?

item.title: Re: The Laughing Tree
item.content:encoded:
Hello Granite Mask! let's take a look :)

SAND

+ show block
– hide block

The concept:

Elevator Pitch: The Laughing Tree is a memetic entity rooted within an old wooden-tent campground commonly used by scouts or youth groups. The tree convinces children to tie one of their own to its trunk, and, when secured, it carves their name into its bark. These victims live on to help the tree find new prey, and laugh at the pain and suffering it inflicts.

Overall the premise sounds interesting, however, theres a few things that don't really add up to me (or aren't really explained/clarified). They're as follows;

-how exactly does the tree convince you to tie someone up?
-does the tree itself carve the child's name? or do the children do it?
-how exactly do these victims live on to help the tree? do they still have any free will? what happens when they leave the camp?
-what exactly happens after ones name is carved?- in the immediate future?

The narratives:

Central Narrative: A low-level foundation member calls attention to this entity after his child is almost tied to the tree. Previously, he laughed the tale off as a "camp story" to several co-workers. He is told to bring in his child for questioning.

This makes me a bit skeptical, the Foundation doesn't really have any real reason to believe this is an anomaly. Especially considering they how little context they have.

The first interiew is short. After providing a cover story, the investigator asks what happened. Emotionally dead, the kid replies, "you'll just laugh at me.."

The second interview occurs after the parent tries to console their child. The parent tries to help answer preliminary questions by describing camp activities. At the mention of knot-tying, the child's face goes pale.

The interviewer asks why the child didn't want to attend camp. Instead of answering he makes a small digression about how he felt sorry for a spider that another kid burned. The parent interrupts and describes his child as socially isolated and wonders if this behavior is related to the nature of his work.
Eventually, the child describes what happened, One-by-one belongings of other scouts find their way to his tent. Camp staff approach him and ask about thefts. The child tries to explain that he didn't take them, but they laugh at him. He runs. Then they, the scouts, surround him with knot-tying ropes and force him toward the *tree*. He tries and fails to escape, as the scouts laugh at him. The laughter grows louder.. and deeper until he realizes that it's coming from behind. And then, the laughter stops.
The investigator asks what changed. The child looks down, and cries.

A third interview takes place with a camp staff-member and former victim. He laughs at suffering he causes, and his own.

As it stands, it feels like I'm missing a fair amount of context here? there isn't really much of an answer to how/why the child didn't want to go to camp (were they aware something was wrong beforehand?), how/why items kept making their way to his tent (was this planned?), and what exactly happened after he got tied to the tree.- the narrative seems to imply his name got carved, but then there are also parts of the narrative that show the parent stepped in?

Red or green?
Due to my afore mentioned concerns, no-greenlight

If you would like me review this (or any concept) further, then please feel free to send me a PM or ask via IRC. Response times generally range from 2-12 hours over PMs. If your looking for additional critiques/perspectives in general, then I would highly recommend contacting the butterfly squad :)

Cheers,

RED


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Laughing Tree

item.title: [Seeking Greenlight] Burning Rubber on the Mind
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: What the summary says pretty much. We have an anomalous version of the Fasciola hepatica parasitic fluke. It worms its way into brains, and makes its hosts zombie-like in their pursuit of rubber. Plenty of rubber-related puns and jokes nestled in there (not super cheesy) for bonus containment points.

Central Narrative: Most of the narrative centers around the progression of the infection and symptom development. I also threw in an addendum about an outbreak at a NASCAR race which I think merges the humor of zombies at a raceway with the detached view of The Foundation in a humorous way.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: There are honestly relatively few biological SCPs that aren't extremely different from real world creatures. I understand why, because doing something that feels realistic is definitely more difficult and takes some of the fun out of the fantasy aspect. However, I think I could fill the lack of high-realism bio-SCPs if I work at it hard enough. I get big into the details of scientific stuff like this, so I think I can pull it off really well.

Additional Notes: I have already written up the entire page. I felt I could only elaborate on the realism aspect I am going for by doing the research and writing the whole thing to make sure it fit nicely. I know there are rules about posting the sandbox links unprompted, so if you are interested in seeing what I've done just ask.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: [Seeking Greenlight] Burning Rubber on the Mind

I didn't really enjoy this piece very much. The sexual part seemed to not really serve much purpose and seemed to be more about shock value than anything else.

-1, not a fan.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-3380

item.title: Re: SCP-XXXX A blessed exchange
item.content:encoded:
Hi Sonderance, thanks for the feedback!

So address your points in order of easiest to hardest.

Number of testing logs
This in and of itself is fine. I have written 9 so far, and my strength will be writing the procedural logs themselves, so this is less a concern for me. The justification for them (covered below) is trickier.

Description
My WIP photoshopped image is here https://i.imgur.com/amUpFVk.png. The shortened object description is essentially a 5.5in wide stone disk with engravings suggesting non-human origin and the symbols are meant to be a form of "universal" description of the object.

Origin of objects
My solution to this is that all the objects created are all "specific" to people/lifeforms and occasionally, the objects will be from different time periods (suggesting they were taken from someone who used it long ago). So no furniture, food, vehicles etc, and items more like clothing, immediate possessions, intimate things like memories/beliefs etc. For example one testing log will have an office briefcase containing secret documents from 50 years ago (something more likely to have disappeared/reappeared than created from thin air). Also toying with the idea of entire identities being taken/returned. I also want to keep purpose of the SCP somewhat mysterious, but it would basically be a semi-sentient "collector".

Testing justification
This is tricky, and something I haven't given an enormous amount of thought (so your feedback is very helpful :) ). My process for the logs so far is that early testing is only dangerous to the tester (creating/removing limbs etc) therefore continued testing is easier to justify as it mostly just maims/kills D class at worst. A possible solution is for the SCP to require periodic exchanges and with them, it compels people to use it (therefore necessitating testing). This fits with my purpose/origin idea but is definitely a bit of a cop out. I can think on this more.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP-XXXX A blessed exchange

Unless they already successfully did. When was the last time anyone saw Thor anyway?


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-2743

Thank you, my friend. Kind words mean alot on this one!


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5923

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Purgeitinflame, per the Ideas Critique forum required reading, thread bumps are not allowed.

The required reading also states:

Authors are expected to reach out to reviewers to seek feedback. See this thread for further information: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Any attempt to use this product in a manner inconsistent with it's labeling...

I remember reading this when it was in the draft stage, and I'm happy to see it now on the mainlist. It's very good, and very heartfelt.

I said it before, I'll say it again. Well done.

+1

item.title: Re: Gossip Ghost
item.content:encoded:
After a conversation on IRC where it was made clear that this idea has been thoroughly thought-through, and specific moods, 'points', goals and approaches have been fleshed out, I'm satisfied enough to give this its first greenlight. Good luck!


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Gossip Ghost

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Moved to Media Discussion.

Also, if you want people to be interested in looking at the podcast, you should mention what the subject matter/premise of it is rather than just saying "it's got good discussion" (which most people would probably assume is a minimum requirement).


Forum category: General Discussion / Media Discussion
Forum thread: A REALLY underrated podcast

item.title: Re: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
What Kara said, and in addition:

I have only ever seen one green light go out throughout the tens of ideas I have over viewed.

There are a few things to consider for why this is the case. We get hundreds of new site members every week, and a lot of them want to write for the site but are unfamiliar with the expectations of the voting audience. Also, a lot of the concepts posted are first-time ideas that pretty much always need some editing before a reviewer would be interested in reading a full draft of the material. Some authors never respond to feedback at all. Lastly, a common trend for authors, particularly newer authors, is to stop working on a concept if it receives a negative review, rather than making edits to improve the concept.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?

YOUR HEART IS NOW BEATING MANUALLY

that actually gave me a tiny heart attack +1


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5922

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Since this thread was on page 6 of the forum listing with no responses, this is a quick staff reminder to the author that if you haven't yet, you should contact reviewers directly to request feedback as needed.

Please refer to this thread for more information: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews

If you have already reached out to multiple reviewers and received no reply, notify a staff member for assistance finding a reviewer.

Forum thread: Systems of Reality

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Noting that this user has created 2 other concept threads within the past 7 days:

  • http://www.scpwiki.com/forum/t-13969087/seeking-greenlights-intergalactic-flushing-service#post-4861696 (3 days ago)
  • http://www.scpwiki.com/forum/t-13970400/seeking-greenlights-retribution-gun#post-4862660 (4 days ago)

Traloc_Dheckoa, per the forum required reading found here:http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282684/required-reading:how-to-use-this-forum-ideas,

Each author is limited to posting two new concept threads per week (every 7 days).

This is to encourage authors to commit to developing the concepts they are asking reviewers to dedicate time to critiquing, rather than just quickly posting and discarding ideas.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: [Seeking Greenlights]Traffic Disappearance

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Please collapse images in the forums. Thank you.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: suadeant (seeking greenlights)

I think skips that've been neutralized no longer require containment of any sort, either because they've become 100% non-anomalous or been completely destroyed. While none of the anomalous functions of the exclusion harness remain functional, the Foundation can't just melt it down or throw it away because it's made of classified Foundation tech and contains sensitive information about some kind of crazy VK-class event or something. It still needs to be kept on hand for future reference and out of the hands of enemies, which is still containment of a sort, so it's considered Safe.

That's my two cents, anyway.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5000

I do not upvote many articles in a year; this is one of them.

P.S.: I'll be pissed if I don't get my dose of articles like this every month. Surely you have some more you can spare?


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5607

item.title: Re: suadeant (seeking greenlights)
item.content:encoded:
I have added a photo that i think would be appropriate for my suggestion! I have also updated the hook, it's something that i had thought about but had not wrotten here.

I am looking into SCP's that might be a good bridge. I know this suggestion is somewhat similar to the "possesive mask" (im not as good at remembering numbers as i am at remembering the stories haha) but i am looking through the 5 and 6 thousand ranges ^^


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: suadeant (seeking greenlights)

I liked this concept of SCP that goes into other fictional media, no matter if it is a movie, cartoon, videogame, stage play… And the way more tests are made keeps the concept fresh for new possibilities.

Too bad I didnt see mention of any Yoko Taro game, bad things usually happen to everyone.

Unless…


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-3922

item.title: Re: The Friendly Shadow
item.content:encoded:
Got it… Sorry. This is my first time writing anything. So, I just need to continue working on execution? Perhaps remove -2 completely? Rewrite the interviews and security footage? I'm overall frustrated because I have no idea how to tackle this. I want to make this good, having read all of the guides regarding humanoids, but nothing is clicking in my brain. Do you have any further advice, or is it sick or swim?

Edit: Sorry again. Baby steps. I'll edit the things you mentioned in your crit. You've also inspired me on how to characterize them in the discovery portion. Thanks!


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Drafts Critique
Forum thread: The Friendly Shadow

item.title: [Seeking Greenlights]Traffic Disappearance
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a phenomenon that occurs in the town of [REDACTED]. When a car passes an unmonitored road alone during the night, a green car of no number plate(SCP-XXXX-1) will manifest nearby and run into a car accident with it. Afterwards, SCP-XXXX-1, and whoever was in the car involved with the accident, will disappear.

Central Narrative: A journalist witnessed an SCP-XXXX event while researching for mysterious disappearance of citizens in traffic accidents. She collected information of every incident that might be related to SCP-XXXX and tried to come up with a scientific explanation to it. However, she was deceased in a car accident by SCP-XXXX a week later. Afterwards, the foundation was able to find a file in which the journalist recorded all of her foundings.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: [Seeking Greenlights]Traffic Disappearance

I really think that's the most underrated SCP. I loved everything about it. Obvious +1.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5997

Hold on. Why is this safe and not neutralized? like seriously it's anomalous effects are gone.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5000

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I’m Zane, and I’ve been reading for several years, but only recently realized I could join the site. I don’t know what else to put here, except I want to write about a diner that if you eat the cheesecake, you get sent to another dimension. I also want to try to get into the 914 test logs. See you on the other side,
-Z


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
